commands.command: build-image
commands.helpInvocation: build-image --help

result.stdout:
Usage: cc build-image <plugin-project-dir> [options]

Builds a Docker container image for a container plugin.
The image name and tag are derived from the plugin manifest's
ContainerImageUri property (e.g. "movies-container-plugin:1.0.0").

Options:
  --image <name:tag>    Override the image name/tag from manifest
                        (e.g. myregistry.azurecr.io/plugins/movies:1.0.0)
  --skip-build          Skip 'dotnet build' (use if already built)

Prerequisites:
  - Docker must be running
  - Plugin must have ExecutionMode = Container in its manifest
  - Plugin .csproj must include <PublishProfile>DefaultContainer</PublishProfile>

Usage: cc generate-manifest <plugin-project-dir> [--output <path>] [--overlay <path>] [--headless]

Extracts the plugin manifest from the compiled DLL and writes
extension.manifest.json in the feature.manifest.json format.

The plugin must be built first (dotnet build). This command does
NOT trigger a build to avoid recursion when called from .targets.

Options:
  --output <path>     Output path for extension.manifest.json
                      (default: <build-output>/extension.manifest.json)
  --overlay <path>    Optional overlay JSON for UI contributions and
                      other metadata not in the SDK PluginManifest
  --headless          Suppress warning for missing UI contributions

commands.command: package
commands.helpInvocation: package --help

result.stdout:
Usage: cc package <plugin-project-dir> [--output <path>] [--key-dir <path>] [--skip-ui] [--skip-image] [--image <name:tag>] [--headless]

Builds the plugin, creates a signed .ccpkg package.
If a ui/ directory with a package.json is found, the frontend is
built automatically and the output is included in the .ccpkg.
For container plugins, the Docker image is built automatically unless --skip-image is set.

Options:
  --output <path>     Output path for the .ccpkg file
  --key-dir <path>    Directory for RSA key pair (default: /tmp/inspectra-help-ethisyscore.cli-1.0.0-alpha.24-265bbe4868834ae18e43218b6c524073/home/.ethisyscore/keys)
  --skip-ui           Skip building and bundling the frontend UI
  --skip-image        Skip Docker image building for container plugins
  --image <name:tag>  Override the container image name/tag from manifest
  --headless          Package a backend-only extension without UI contributions

Signing:
  All plugins share a single RSA-2048 key pair. The private key signs
  packages; the matching public key is embedded inside the .ccpkg.
  The server verifies signatures using the embedded key and stores it
  per-extension on first install (Trust On First Use). Subsequent
  upgrades must be signed with the same key.

commands.payload:
Usage: cc package <plugin-project-dir> [--output <path>] [--key-dir <path>] [--skip-ui] [--skip-image] [--image <name:tag>] [--headless]

Builds the plugin, creates a signed .ccpkg package.
If a ui/ directory with a package.json is found, the frontend is
built automatically and the output is included in the .ccpkg.
For container plugins, the Docker image is built automatically unless --skip-image is set.

Options:
  --output <path>     Output path for the .ccpkg file
  --key-dir <path>    Directory for RSA key pair (default: /tmp/inspectra-help-ethisyscore.cli-1.0.0-alpha.24-265bbe4868834ae18e43218b6c524073/home/.ethisyscore/keys)
  --skip-ui           Skip building and bundling the frontend UI
  --skip-image        Skip Docker image building for container plugins
  --image <name:tag>  Override the container image name/tag from manifest
  --headless          Package a backend-only extension without UI contributions

Signing:
  All plugins share a single RSA-2048 key pair. The private key signs
  packages; the matching public key is embedded inside the .ccpkg.
  The server verifies signatures using the embedded key and stores it
  per-extension on first install (Trust On First Use). Subsequent
  upgrades must be signed with the same key.

EthisysCore CLI v0.0.0+fc5015817501a0bad778e09d9b840af96c13f273

Usage:
  cc generate feature <name> [--type=<platform-app|tenant-app>] [--template=<html|react|vue>] [--layered] [--mode=<in-process|out-of-process|container>] [--internal]
  cc generate from-schema <schema.json> [--name=<name>] [--type=<platform-app|tenant-app>] [--internal]
  cc generate-manifest <plugin-project-dir> [--output <path>] [--overlay <path>]
  cc package <plugin-project-dir> [--output <path>] [--key-dir <path>] [--skip-image] [--image <name:tag>]
  cc build-image <plugin-project-dir> [--image <name:tag>] [--skip-build]
  cc sandbox [--plugin-path <path>]

Commands:
  generate feature      Scaffold a new plugin project (optionally with UI template)
  generate from-schema  Generate a compiled plugin from a DynamicApp JSON schema
  generate-manifest     Extract manifest from compiled DLL and write extension.manifest.json
  package               Build, bundle, and sign a .ccpkg package (auto-builds Docker image for container plugins)
  build-image           Build a Docker container image for a container plugin
  sandbox               Launch the local development environment (Aspire)

Flags:
  --version, -v         Print the CLI version
  --internal            Use ProjectReference to EthisysCore.Plugin.Sdk (for developers
                        working inside the EthisysCore repo). This is the default.
                        Omit this flag for external clients who use the NuGet package.
  --template            UI framework template: html, react, or vue
  --mode                Execution mode: in-process (default), out-of-process, or container
  --layered             Use 4-project Clean Architecture (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, Plugin)
